description: Make ML pipeline production-ready by connecting naive JS implementations to real inference backends (ONNX Runtime, external embedding APIs, Triton client). Replace hardcoded JS algorithms with proper ML service clients.
steps: 1. Upgrade packages/ml-pipeline/src/core/embedding-store.ts - Replace the in-memory vector storage with a proper VectorStore interface that uses @quant/search VectorClient (Qdrant). Keep an in-memory fallback for tests. The VectorClient already exists in packages/search and is used by search-indexer.

2. Upgrade packages/ml-pipeline/src/core/spam-classifier.ts - Replace the naive Bayes JS implementation with a SpamClassifier that uses the ML runtime. When ONNX model is available (via ml-runtime ModelLoader), use real inference. When not, keep the current naive implementation as fallback. Add SpamClassificationResult type with confidence score.

3. Upgrade packages/ml-pipeline/src/core/sentiment-analyzer.ts - Replace lexicon-based scoring with a SentimentAnalyzer that calls the AI engine for real sentiment analysis (using @quant/ai UnifiedAIService.generateText with a sentiment prompt). Keep lexicon fallback for offline/no-API scenarios.
4. Upgrade packages/ml-pipeline/src/core/ner-engine.ts - Replace regex/dictionary NER with an LLM-based NER that uses structured output from @quant/ai to extract entities. Keep regex fallback.

5. Upgrade packages/recommendations/src/core/neural-cf.ts - The file already mentions Triton fallback. Ensure it properly uses packages/triton-client to call Triton Inference Server when TRITON_URL env var is set. Wire the TritonClient for real gRPC/HTTP inference calls.

6. Connect packages/triton-client properly - verify it has a real HTTP/gRPC client for Triton inference (not just types). If stub, implement a real TritonInferenceClient that calls POST /v2/models/{model}/infer.
7. Add proper tests for all upgraded modules with mocked backends.

8. Verify: pnpm turbo typecheck --filter=@quant/ml-pipeline --filter=@quant/recommendations --filter=@quant/triton-client && pnpm turbo test --filter=@quant/ml-pipeline --filter=@quant/recommendations
acceptance_criteria: Embedding store uses Qdrant VectorClient when available
Spam classifier uses ONNX model via ml-runtime when available
Sentiment analyzer uses real AI for analysis when API keys are set
NER engine uses LLM-based extraction when AI service available
Neural CF calls Triton server when TRITON_URL is configured
All implementations gracefully fall back to existing naive methods when services unavailable
findings: All implementations use a backend/provider interface pattern: VectorStoreBackend for embedding store (Qdrant), SpamModelBackend for spam classifier (ONNX), SentimentBackend for sentiment analyzer (AI service), NERBackend for NER engine (LLM). Each gracefully falls back to existing naive implementation on error or when backend is unavailable. NeuralCF already had proper Triton integration - just removed @simulated annotation. Added @quant/ai and @quant/search as workspace deps to ml-pipeline. All 464 tests pass (220 ml-pipeline + 235 recommendations + 9 triton-client). Lint and typecheck clean.
